description_long: This flower features a prominent, elongated green central structure resembling a stylized stem or pistil, composed of several rounded segments stacked vertically. Flanking this central axis are two large, symmetrical wings or petal clusters, each formed by a series of delicate, curved, wire-like elements that radiate outward and upward, creating a sense of airy lightness. The petals display a gradient of colors, transitioning from a silvery white at the tips to a brownish hue near the base, with a subtle blue tint along the outer edges. The overall form is highly symmetrical, with the petal clusters mirroring each other on either side of the central green structure. No leaves are visible, and the base of the flower is not clearly defined, giving it a floating or suspended appearance. The design is both organic and abstract, evoking the image of a butterfly or a fantastical blossom.
description: A symmetrical flower with a tall green central axis and two large, airy, wire-like petal clusters in silvery, brown, and blue hues. The structure is delicate and abstract, resembling a butterfly.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full symmetry of the flower, with both petal clusters and the green central axis clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the thinness of the flower, with the petal clusters appearing as narrow lines and the green axis as a vertical strip.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, displaying the symmetrical petal arrangement and the green central structure from behind.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, highlighting the slender profile and layered petal structure.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged bilaterally in two large, symmetrical clusters on either side of the central axis, with each cluster composed of multiple elongated, wire-like petals radiating outward and slightly upward. The arrangement is highly symmetrical and layered, resembling butterfly wings.
